lockingPolicy.lockReason: Regression scenarios encode the SST single-ingestion-point contract for the QF-decisions callback signing keystore: the keystore source is Config (not os.Getenv) and Validate() catches malformed JSON at boot. Any future change to the scenario set requires explicit invalidation evidence per Gate G058/G059.

tdd.rationale: Real code change: Config field addition + Load + Validate wiring + keystore loader API change + 4 existing call-site migrations + 3 NEW regression tests. NOT artifact-only. Red->Green evidence is mandatory.
tdd.redGreenEvidence: Red: the new BUG020010 tests authored against main MUST FAIL — tests as build failures (the QFDecisionsCallbackSigningKeysJSON Config field and parsed.CallbackSigningKeysJSON QFConfig field do not exist). Green: after the migration, all BUG020010 tests PASS and the migrated existing call sites still PASS.

scenarios.title: Keystore is populated from Config, not from process environment (adversarial — env unset)
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Use t.Setenv to clear QF_DECISIONS_CALLBACK_SIGNING_KEYS_JSON. Construct a ConnectorConfig with SourceConfig['callback_signing_keys_json'] set to a valid 1-key JSON string. Call parseConfig then LoadCallbackKeystoreFromJSON(parsed.CallbackSigningKeysJSON). Assert keystore is non-nil and keystore.KeyIDs() contains the expected key_id. Adversarial: if the loader still read from os.Getenv, the empty env would yield a nil keystore.

scenarios.title: Validate() fails loud on a malformed signing-keys JSON
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: cfg.QFDecisionsEnabled=true; other required QF-decisions fields set to valid values; cfg.QFDecisionsCallbackSigningKeysJSON='not-valid-json'; call Load() which invokes Validate(); assert non-nil error AND message contains QF_DECISIONS_CALLBACK_SIGNING_KEYS_JSON.

scenarios.title: Validate() permits empty signing-keys JSON when QF decisions is enabled (PERMISSIVE policy)
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: cfg.QFDecisionsEnabled=true; other required QF-decisions fields set to valid values; QF_DECISIONS_CALLBACK_SIGNING_KEYS_JSON=''; call Load(); assert nil error. PERMISSIVE policy finalized in report.md Naming Decision.

scenarios.title: os.Getenv literal is eradicated from the keystore source
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: exec.Command('grep', '-nE', 'os\.Getenv\(', 'internal/connector/qfdecisions/callback_keystore.go'); assert exit code 1 (no matches). Also asserts the file no longer imports "os". Permanent structural regression guard mirroring the BUG-020-008 and BUG-020-009 helper-eradication grep tests.

scenarios.title: Existing integration call sites still pass after migration
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: go test -tags=integration ./tests/integration/ -run TestQFCallback -count=1; assert exit 0. The 3 existing call sites (qf_callback_signing_test.go L76, qf_watch_proposal_test.go L91 + L198) that previously called LoadCallbackKeystoreFromEnv() are migrated to LoadCallbackKeystoreFromJSON directly; semantics preserved; suite continues to exercise the QF callback signing flow end-to-end against the live test stack.

scenarios.title: Build and unit test suite pass after the fix
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: go build ./... exit 0; ./smackerel.sh test unit PASSES for every touched package (internal/config, internal/connector/qfdecisions, cmd/core). The pre-existing FAIL in internal/connector/keep is unrelated spec 059 work-in-progress (confirmed by git status not touching keep/).
